al Phase Driven Algorithm for the Constrained Hardware/Software Partitioning Problem Asawaree Kalavade and Edward A. Lee Proceedings of Codes/CASHE '94, Third Intl. Workshop on Hardware/Software Codesign Grenoble, France, Sept. 22-24, 1994, pp 42-48

ign Grenoble, France, Sept. 22-24, 1994, pp 42-48 Prepublished version Published version ABSTRACT An algorithm for the constrained hardware/software partitioning (assignment and scheduling) problem is presented. The key feature of the algorithm is th

presented. The key feature of the algorithm is the adaptive objective mechanism governed by the combination of global and local measures. As hardware area minimization and latency constraints present contradictory objectives, a global time-criticali

contradictory objectives, a global time-criticality (GC) measure selects an objective function in accordance with feasibility. In addition to global consideration, local characteristics of the nodes are emphasized by classifying nodes into local pha

are emphasized by classifying nodes into local phase (LP) types. A local phase 1 node (extremity) has an obvious preference for an implementation on the basis of its area/time requirements. A local phase 2 node (repeller) is a repeller to an implemen

ase 2 node (repeller) is a repeller to an implementation on the basis of relative preferences of other nodes. At each iteration, the global and local criteria are superimposed by a threshold mechanism so as to determine the best implementation. The a
so as to determine the best implementation. The algorithm has quadratic complexity in the number of nodes and has shown promising behavior on the examples tested.
